migrated_components.note: v2 exported as Tag from badge-v2.tsx
v1 uses radio-group.css, v2 uses radio-v2.css

v2_only_components.name: MenuV2 (namespace)
v2_only_components.note: v1 had dropdown-menu.css + context-menu.css but no tsx component

v2_only_components.name: FieldV2 / Field
v2_only_components.note: New layout component with no v1 equivalent

v2_only_components.name: SegmentedControlV2
v2_only_components.note: New component with no v1 equivalent

v2_only_components.name: Icon (sprite)
v2_only_components.note: Different icon system — v1 uses separate SVG files

v2_only_components.name: WordmarkV2
v2_only_components.note: SVG logo with no v1 equivalent

duplicate_css_layers.analysis: Both v1 and v2 use the same CSS layer name 'theme' but are structured completely differently with entirely separate custom properties.

v2_layers.layer_order: @layer theme (no explicit layer order in v2)

duplicate_layer_names.risk: If both v1/index.css and v2 CSS are loaded on the same page, both sets of --v2-* and non-prefixed custom properties co-exist, but the layer cascade may conflict if v2's theme.css is loaded outside v1's layer ordering

duplicate_css_layers.conflicting_properties_assessment: No direct conflicts: v1 uses unprefixed tokens (--background-base), v2 uses --v2- prefixed tokens. However, v1 theme.css defines ~350 CSS custom properties, v2 theme.css defines another ~100 (with duplicates in light/dark blocks). Both loaded = memory overhead but no cascade conflict.

duplicate_icon_sets.analysis: Two completely separate icon systems exist side by side with no overlap.

v2_icon_system.format: Inline SVG string definitions rendered into an <svg><symbol> sprite at runtime via ensureSprite()
v2_icon_system.note: The v2 Icon's 'name' prop accepts 'string & {}' so technically any string is accepted at compile-time, but only 14 defined icons render. Unknown names fall back to 'plus'.

duplicate_icon_sets.note: The two systems are entirely separate — different locations, different rendering strategies, different icon sets. v2 has far fewer icons (14) compared to v1's unlimited external SVGs.

v1_pattern.adoption: All 54 v1 CSS files use data-component/data-slot selectors. No className-based selectors found in CSS (though some tsx files may pass class via className).
v1_pattern.custom_properties: Unprefixed: --background-base, --text-strong, --icon-base, etc. Uses --font-family-sans, --font-size-small, etc. from theme.css.

v2_pattern.selector_format: data-component and data-slot attribute selectors (same pattern as v1!)
v2_pattern.note: Same selector convention as v1 — just different component names. No className-based selectors anywhere.

css_selector_patterns.distinction: The primary pattern difference is the custom property naming: v1 uses unprefixed tokens for everything (--background-base), v2 uses --v2- prefix (--v2-background-bg-base). The data-component/ data-slot attribute selector convention is identical between v1 and v2 — no architectural shift in CSS methodology.

consumption_pattern.v1_styles_import: import '@opencode-ai/ui/styles' (loads index.css with all 54 component CSS files + theme)
consumption_pattern.v2_styles_import: import '@opencode-ai/ui/v2/styles/tailwind' (loads colors.css + theme.css only — no component CSS)
consumption_pattern.v2_component_css_import: Each v2 component imports its own CSS file locally (import './component-v2.css')
consumption_pattern.note: There are NO index.ts barrel files at v2/ root or v2/components/. All imports are direct file-path

v1.colors_file: styles/colors.css (773 lines) — 12 raw palette sets × light/dark with legacy aliases (smoke, yuzu, cobalt, apple, ember, solaris, lilac, coral, mint, blue, ink, amber)
v1.theme_file: styles/theme.css (610 lines) — flat CSS custom properties with @media (prefers-color-scheme) dark overrides

v2.colors_file: v2/styles/colors.css (172 lines) — 9 raw palette sets (grey, red, orange, yellow, green, cyan, blue, purple, pink) + alpha-dark/alpha-light scales
v2.theme_file: v2/styles/theme.css (378 lines) — structured semantic tokens (background, text, icon, border, overlay, state, elevation, illustration) with separated [data-color-scheme='light'] and [data-color-scheme='dark'] attribute selectors
v2.color_scheme_control: Explicit [data-color-scheme] HTML attribute (JS-controlled, no media query fallback — the OS media query block is commented out)

theme_system_comparison.key_difference: v2 has a flatter token structure (no SASS-like nesting) and avoids @media query for dark mode in favor of [data-color-scheme] attribute switching. v2 tokens are --v2- prefixed to co-exist with v1 tokens.

recommendations.description: Add barrel export files: v2/index.ts and v2/components/index.ts to enable clean imports like `import { ButtonV2 } from '@opencode-ai/ui/v2'` instead of direct file paths.

V1's styles/index.css loads ALL 54 component CSS files on every import via @import. As v2 components replace v1 usage, prune unused v1 CSS imports to reduce bundle size. Currently there's no mechanism to load only needed v1 component CSS.

V2 component CSS is imported per-component via `import './component-v2.css'` in each .tsx file. This means if v2 is used alongside v1, the v1 styles/index.css is still loaded separately. Consider whether v2 component CSS should be loaded via the same global layer mechanism.

Two entirely separate icon systems exist: v1's external SVG file approach (unlimited icons via files) vs v2's inline SVG sprite (14 icons hard-coded). They should be consolidated into a single system, likely the sprite approach with all icons migrated.

34 v1 components still lack v2 counterparts, including: card, markdown, popover, progress, spinner, scroll-view, list, and many app-specific components (message-part, session-review, shell-submessage, etc.). These need v2 equivalents or a decision to keep them v1-only.

V2 CSS files declare `@layer theme` but do NOT participate in the same layer cascade as v1's `@layer theme, base, components, utilities`. If both are loaded, v2's styles appear in the `theme` layer (lowest priority) regardless of where the loader imports them. This may cause unintended overrides.
badge-v2.tsx exports `Tag` not `Badge`. V1 has a `tag` component with tag.css. This naming inconsistency between the file name (badge) and the export (Tag) could cause confusion.
26 v2 .stories.tsx files accompany components. If migrating from v1, existing v1 stories should be removed or consolidated to avoid having parallel story files for the same component.

pre_existing_findings_cross_reference.summary: @opencode-ai/core uses wildcard export with no public API boundary
pre_existing_findings_cross_reference.relevance: Same concern applies to @opencode-ai/ui's wildcard exports (./* → ./src/components/*.tsx)
